programmes.show_description: Vauxhall Victor FB / Volvo Amazon VB: Derek picks up a five-vehicle haul from a collector who can no longer look after his motors, and Paul collects a rare 1972 Lancia Fulvia Berlina.
Alfa Romeo Giulia/Citroen 2CV6: Derek casts his eye over a Citroen 2CV, before he is off admiring the treasure trove of more than 270 motorbikes in the same garage.
Ford Zephyr/Autobianchi: Derek heads off to collect some vintage motorcycles from a garage at the bottom of the garden in the North-East of England.

Fiat Strada Abarth/Austin 10: Dave finds two rare Fiats and a VW Corrado. Derek has his work cut out trying to remove an old Morris that appears to be supporting the garage roof. What could possibly g
Shopping from Home.
Taunton to Newton Abbot: Michael Portillo travels between Taunton and Newton Abbot, joining in with a fertility dance in Stogumber, and visiting a celebrated garden at Hestercombe.
Plymouth to The Lizard: Travelling from Plymouth to the Lizard, Michael Portillo investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic.
Hull to Malton: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from East Yorkshire to Caernarfon, beginning by travelling from Hull to Malton.
York to Frizinghall: Michael Portillo conducts important research in an historic York tea room, discovers Leeds' textile heritage, and investigates Bradford's musical heritage.
Austin Morris Minor: Up for sale at the Mathewson's auction is a 1275 Mini GT that's come from just down the road and could be a future British icon.
Swift/Toyota Corona/Bedford Van: Derek goes to pick up a veteran 1904 Swift, there's an emotional farewell to a 1980s Bedford HA van, and a Toyota Corona finds a new home.
Vernichtungskrieg: Adolf Hitler has his mind on making Germany into a great empire by expanding eastwards, but his invasion of Poland tips the world into a violent and bloody war.
The Reckoning: Following the fight to hunt down Nazi war criminals in the 1960s and 70s, such as Beate Klarsfeld's determination to out Kurt Kiesinger.
Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden in Edinburgh. Items brought in to be valued include rare Russian jewels.
Aston Hall 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Aston Hall in Birmingham, and is joined by poet Benjamin Zephaniah, who has fond childhood memories of playing in the grounds.
E Type S2/Escort RS Turbo: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing.
Rolls Silver Shadow/Austin 7: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.
Vernichtungskrieg: Adolf Hitler has his mind on making Germany into a great empire by expanding eastwards, but his invasion of Poland tips the world into a violent and bloody war.
The Reckoning: Following the fight to hunt down Nazi war criminals in the 1960s and 70s, such as Beate Klarsfeld's determination to out Kurt Kiesinger.
Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden in Edinburgh. Items brought in to be valued include rare Russian jewels.
Patagonia: Simon Reeve treks across the high mountains and treacherous ice fields of Patagonia, getting close to pumas and riding with gauchos, the cowboys of South America.
Dom builds a drift car with engineer Sam Lovegrove. At home, he gets a digger in to make his new pond, and he and Sam have a go at gig rowing after fixing the club trailer in Hayle.
Simon Reeve presents highlights of his journeys around the world, and catches up with the people he met, including a Burmese undercover human rights campaigner.
E Type S2/Escort RS Turbo: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing.
Rolls Silver Shadow/Austin 7: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.
Atari: Shamil buys an '80s Atari which is a more complex fix than it first appears. Rob needs to unravel a previous owner's modifications before he can get it working again.
Sinclair C5: Shamil's inspired to buy an original 1980s Sinclair C5 for Rob to repair.
Camping Trailer and Two-Seater Toboggan: Dr Shini and her team of makers and gadgeteers are tasked with reinventing a camping trailer from 1950 and a two-seater toboggan from 1960.
Shopping from Home.
Sheffield to Nantwich: Michael Portillo travels to the steel city of Sheffield where he discovers an early 20th century steam engine, before heading to Nantwich.
Liverpool to Dolgarrog: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales, finding a miniature tank engine made by Frank Hornby and visiting the ruins of Gwrych Castle.
Criccieth to Caernarfon: In Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - 1,500 feet across a quarry - as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon.
Warrington to Preston: Michael Portillo sets off on a trip through Britain's industrial heartlands in the footsteps of George V, beginning by travelling from Warrington to Preston.
MGYB / Yamaha / Quattro: Derek gives the once-over to a Royale Formula Ford race car, and Paul is on a marathon trip to pick up a 1953 MGYB, a Moggy Van, and an '80s Yamaha 250RD motorbike.
Thunderbird / Citroen SM / Punto / BMW: Derek travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave admires a two-decade-old Punto with just 42 miles on the clock.
A Superhuman Act of Inhumanity: Betrayed, Stalin fights the German army in the Soviet states. The German occupation policies lead to the first extermination policies of 'undesirables'.
Paranoia: Two spies turn double agents at the height of the Cold War standoff between East and West. Their deception happens as the superpowers edge towards nuclear war.
Portchester Castle 2: The Roadshow travels to Portchester Castle in Hampshire, where treasures include punk clothing, and paintings by 20th-century Indian artists.
Ulster 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Ulster Folk Museum near Belfast, where treasures include stained glass, and a bell from a Japanese temple.
Astra: A sun-bleached red Vauxhall Astra GTE gives the team a headache as they also buy a classic French moped and catch up on the restoration of a barn find Mini Cooper S.
Vanden Plas: The team gamble on restoring a Vanden Plas saloon from the 1970s and a child's tricycle from the 1960s while catching up on the restoration of a rare Sunbeam Lotus.
A Superhuman Act of Inhumanity: Betrayed, Stalin fights the German army in the Soviet states. The German occupation policies lead to the first extermination policies of 'undesirables'.
Paranoia: Two spies turn double agents at the height of the Cold War standoff between East and West. Their deception happens as the superpowers edge towards nuclear war.
Portchester Castle 2: The Roadshow travels to Portchester Castle in Hampshire, where treasures include punk clothing, and paintings by 20th-century Indian artists.
Coral Triangle: Simon Reeve voyages across the Coral Triangle in the south-western Pacific, surrounded by the Philippines, Malaysia and Indonesia.
VW Golf Cabriolet: Derek and Paul are off to Leeds on a rare father and son road trip to pick up a VW Cabriolet and a Lotus Elite.
Austin A30 Seven/Hillman Imp: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors.
Astra: A sun-bleached red Vauxhall Astra GTE gives the team a headache as they also buy a classic French moped and catch up on the restoration of a barn find Mini Cooper S.
